title: Cambridge (Annette's Memorial) & Brixworth/Market Harborough

main_route.excerpt: Simon + [?Tinder] drove across to Cambridge, bad road works on A14. Arrived 1.30. in time for Annette's Memorial lunch.
Then Simon + I set out back westwards to the [?Arlingworth] Lodge Campsite, next to the village.
We set off for home just before 3 in case Naomi needed help after Walter's tongue-tie op.

stops.excerpt: set off on a bike ride a disused railway, all the way to Brixworth where we had fish + chips at the pub. The saxon church 640 AD is very impressive.
We went on another, 10m. bike ride, the other way, to Market Harborough. We met a pleasant Vietnamese & French couple on a bridge.

unresolved.issue: Place name uncertain — transcription unclear; Arlingworth is a hamlet in Northamptonshire near Market Harborough and the Brampton Valley Cyclepath, which fits the context perfectly, but spelling needs verification against the original handwriting.
unresolved.excerpt: Then Simon + I set out back westwards to the [?Arlingworth] Lodge Campsite, next to the village.

boundary_flags.note: Page B_0264_L is mixed: the top portion ('Saturday 15 Aug') belongs to a different trip (Channel Tunnel crossing, Kent, M25, Oxford) and is not part of this trip. The 'Friday 4th Sept' heading marks the start of this trip.

summary: A short domestic UK trip in early September 2015: Simon drove to Cambridge (with Tinder) for a memorial service for Annette at Lucy Cavendish College, while Eve travelled there separately by train from London. Afterwards Simon and Eve drove west to a campsite near Arlingworth, Northamptonshire, from where they made two bike rides along the Brampton Valley Cyclepath — one to Brixworth (with its remarkable Saxon church) and one to Market Harborough — before returning home on Sunday 6 September.
extraction_notes: 1. Page B_0264_L is a mixed-boundary page: the 'Saturday 15 Aug' section at the top belongs to a preceding trip (Channel Tunnel, Kent, M25, Oxford) and has been excluded from this trip's stops. 2. The campsite name reads '[?Arlingworth] Lodge Campsite' — Arlingworth is a plausible match (hamlet in Northamptonshire on or near the Brampton Valley Cyclepath between Brixworth and Market Harborough), but the original handwriting is uncertain. 3. Cambridge is treated as a main_stop rather than an overnight_base because the diary does not explicitly state the party slept in Cambridge; after the memorial they drove westward to the campsite. 4. Eve's arrival in Cambridge pre-dates Friday 4 Sept (she was at the FCO on the Thursday, then travelled by train from London to Cambridge), but the trip is recorded as starting Friday 4 Sept. 5. No ferry or tunnel crossings — entirely domestic GB trip. 6. The date 2015-09-06 for Sunday is confirmed by the ISO date in page B_0265_L.
